Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.8.3359 upgrade for JDH
Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the JDH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalise the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

Title: Email setup
Notes: As per email request received from Donald to create the new credentials for new hire Tushar Dodiya. SO login into AD server , created the credentials and sync the user to O365. After that when tried to assign the license, there were no available licenses. So updated the same to Donald and informed lead for license requirement.

Title: Email setup
Notes: After receiving Donald’s email requesting active user details to review licensing, shared the required information. Upon confirmation from Donald, identified that Chris Campbell was left and assigned the license to tushar@jdhpacific.co.The update was then communicated back to Donald.

Title: Call from Henry regarding Remote access
Notes:
As per the call received from Henry, he was unable to access his office machine from home. I asked him to verify the portal he was using, and found that he was accessing the old URL. I provided him with the updated URL and explained the correct process, but he was still unable to connect and could not create a session due to something blocking the connection on his home machine.
I then requested him to share his screen via Quick Assist. I guided him through the correct access steps, bookmarked the updated URL for easy access, and installed the required agents to enable communication. After completing the setup, the connection worked successfully.

Title: ManageEngine Agent Syncing Issue with the Portal on :-DESKTOP-5RDSJ33, DESKTOP-9SRRKTH, DESKTOP-ED8O2CQ, FRONTDESK-JDH

So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket.
